prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: condensed Fukui functions (most‐electrophilic site f⁺ value reported), units dimensionless
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2‐TZVP (tier ‘standard’ = B3LYP/def2‐TZVP) with RI density fitting (PySCF)
• Geometry provenance: single‐point on fixed geometry supplied by user (‘/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz’); no re‐optimization at DFT (DFT//input‐geom).
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).
• Charge = 0; Multiplicity = 1 (closed‐shell singlet). Cation (+1, doublet) and anion (−1, doublet) states were computed for the N ± 1 steps.
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF backend, Mulliken charges.
• Convergence & caveats: All SCF calculations converged (neutral, cation, anion). integrity.status = "ok", integrity.trustworthy = true.
  Warnings reproduced verbatim below.
• Live log: calculation output was streamed live to a time-stamped `.out` file in the working directory; path available on request.
• Command (from cli_invocation):
  /home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpjmqntk6b/live.json

Headline result
The most electrophilic site in furan is carbon atom 5 (index 4) with Fukui f⁺ = 1.9242931 (dimensionless). The most nucleophilic site is the same carbon atom with f⁻ = 0.1616371.
